skill.name: react-doctor
skill.description: Run after making React changes to catch issues early. Use when reviewing code, finishing a feature, or fixing bugs in a React project.
skill.summary: Run React Doctor after code changes to catch React issues early.
skill.author: millionco

security_audit.summary: The external command finding is a true positive because SKILL.md instructs users to run an npx package from npm using the latest tag. This appears intentional for the skill, but it carries supply-chain and local execution risk. The weak cryptographic algorithm finding at SKILL.md line 3 is a false positive; no cryptographic code or insecure algorithm is present.

medium_findings.title: External Package Execution
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md instructs users to run `npx -y react-doctor@latest . --verbose --diff`. This executes an external npm package on the local codebase and uses the mutable latest tag, which creates supply-chain and local command execution risk. The command is consistent with the skill purpose, so this is not evidence of malicious intent.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is directly present in the usage block and clearly executes an npm package. The context supports legitimate diagnostic use, but the execution risk remains real.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Is False Positive
low_findings.description: The static finding points to the SKILL.md frontmatter description. That line describes when to run the skill and contains no cryptographic operation, algorithm, secret handling, or hash usage. No evidence found for weak cryptography in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is plain descriptive metadata. The reviewed file contains no cryptographic implementation or configuration.

dangerous_patterns.title: npx Command Executes External Code
dangerous_patterns.description: The usage instructions run an npm package through npx. Users should review the package source and prefer pinned versions before running it on sensitive repositories.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is explicit and matches the static external command finding. The risk is contextual rather than malicious.

content.value_statement: React changes can introduce hidden security, performance, correctness, and architecture issues. This skill directs Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to run React Doctor and use its diagnostics for targeted fixes.
actual_capabilities: Runs React Doctor after React code changes.
Checks a React codebase for security, performance, correctness, and architecture issues.
Reports a 0-100 score from the diagnostic tool.
Produces actionable diagnostics for detected issues.
Supports a fix and re-run workflow to verify improvements.
limitations: Requires the external react-doctor npm package to be available.
Executes a local npx command against the current project.
Results depend on the React Doctor package behavior and version.
Does not replace manual review for product logic or design intent.

use_cases.title: Feature Completion Check
use_cases.description: Run diagnostics after a new React feature to catch issues before review.

use_cases.title: Pull Request Review Support
use_cases.description: Use the score and diagnostics to focus review comments on measurable problems.

use_cases.title: Bug Fix Verification
use_cases.description: Re-run the tool after fixes to confirm the score improves and errors are reduced.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Basic React Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Run React Doctor on this project and summarize the main issues in plain language.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner diagnostic run after small React changes.

prompt_templates.title: Review Recent React Changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Run React Doctor using the diff workflow, then list the highest priority issues from my recent React changes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused review before opening a pull request.

prompt_templates.title: Fix and Verify Diagnostics
prompt_templates.prompt: Run React Doctor, fix the most important errors, then re-run it and compare the results.
prompt_templates.scenario: Iterative repair workflow for correctness and performance problems.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Release Quality Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Run React Doctor with verbose output, group findings by risk, and prepare a release readiness summary.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced audit before merging a major React change.

output_examples.input: Run React Doctor after changes to a dashboard component.
output_examples.output: The report identifies performance and correctness issues, ranks the most important fixes, and recommends re-running the tool after changes.

output_examples.input: Use React Doctor during pull request review.
output_examples.output: The assistant summarizes the score, highlights changed-file diagnostics, and turns the results into review comments.

output_examples.input: Verify fixes after resolving React Doctor errors.
output_examples.output: The assistant compares the new score with the previous run and notes which problems remain.

best_practices: Run the skill after meaningful React changes, not only before release.
Fix errors before warnings, then re-run diagnostics to verify progress.
Pin the React Doctor package version when working in sensitive repositories.
anti_patterns: Do not run external npm packages on untrusted repositories without review.
Do not treat the score as a replacement for human code review.
Do not ignore diagnostics that affect security, correctness, or user data.

faq.question: What does this skill run?
faq.answer: It instructs the assistant to run React Doctor through npx on the current React project.

faq.question: Does it change my code automatically?
faq.answer: The skill describes a diagnostic workflow. Code changes depend on the assistant task you request.

faq.question: Why is there a security warning?
faq.answer: The skill uses an external npx command, so users should review and pin the package when needed.

faq.question: Can it check only recent changes?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented command includes a diff mode intended for recent React changes.

faq.question: Is the score enough to approve a release?
faq.answer: No. Use the score as one input alongside tests, review, and product validation.

faq.question: Which assistants can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
